cat > r-curriculum-overview.qmd << 'EOF'
---
title: "r-curriculum"
subtitle: "A Progressive Hands-on Introduction to R"
author: "Troy Altus"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    smooth-scroll: true
    code-tools: true
    theme: cosmo
---

## Overview

This repository provides a structured, progressive curriculum for learning R through practical, well-commented scripts. Each script builds upon the previous ones and focuses on modern, tidyverse-style R.

### Key Features
- Consistent numbering and naming
- Clear learning progression
- Practical exercises in every script
- Uses renv for reproducible package management

## Curriculum Map

| Script | Topic                                      | Main Packages                  | What YouвЂ™ll Learn |
|--------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|-------------------|
| [01](scripts/script-01-r-fundamentals.R)      | R Fundamentals                            | base R                        | Syntax, variables, data types, basic operations |
| [02](scripts/script-02-data-structures-deep-dive.R) | Data Structures                        | base R, tibble                | Vectors, lists, data frames, tibbles |
| [03](scripts/script-03-control-flow-and-functions.R) | Control Flow & Functions             | base R                        | Loops, conditionals, writing reusable functions |
| [04](scripts/script-04-data-import-and-export.R) | Data Import & Export                   | readr, readxl                 | Reading/writing CSV, Excel, RDS |
| [05](scripts/script-05-data-wrangling-with-dplyr-and-tidyr.R) | Data Wrangling                | dplyr, tidyr                  | filter, select, mutate, group_by, pivoting |
| [06](scripts/script-06-ggplot2-visualization.R) | Data Visualization                     | ggplot2                       | Scatter, bar, boxplot, faceting, themes, ggsave() |
| [07](scripts/script-07-data-import-export-and-dates.R) | Advanced Import/Export + Dates     | readr, readxl, writexl, lubridate | Real-world files, date parsing & manipulation |

## How to Use This Curriculum

1. Clone the repository
2. Open the project in RStudio or VS Code
3. Run renv::restore() to install all required packages
4. Work through the scripts in order (they are designed to build on each other)
5. Complete the exercises at the end of each script

## Project Structure

```bash
r-curriculum/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ README.md
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ r-curriculum-overview.qmd
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ _quarto.yml
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .gitignore
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ renv/                  # Package management
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ scripts/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ script-01-r-fundamentals.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ script-02-data-structures-deep-dive.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ script-03-control-flow-and-functions.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ script-04-data-import-and-export.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ script-05-data-wrangling-with-dplyr-and-tidyr.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ script-06-ggplot2-visualization.R
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ script-07-data-import-export-and-dates.R
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ outputs/               # Saved plots and exported files
в””в”Ђв”Ђ data/                  # Sample datasets (optional)
